{"step_by_step_thinking": "Baloxavir marboxil is a cap-dependent endonuclease inhibitor. Document [0] states that it may be considered in cases where oseltamivir resistance is suspected or confirmed in avian influenza, and it can be used in combination therapy in severe cases to reduce viral load. Document [3] specifically mentions that baloxavir has demonstrated efficacy in reducing viral replication of H5N1 and H7N9, which are avian influenza strains. Although the documents do not explicitly state its effectiveness against human influenza, the mechanism and efficacy in avian strains suggest it is effective against influenza in general. The context of use in avian influenza, including combination therapy and reduction of viral load, supports its antiviral activity.
